summary: PR 1438 documented and partially demystified Fox/Falco/Kirby blaster item behavior while adding a full C implementation for the L-Gun item. The FoxBlasterVars struct kept offset-based unknown fields but gained semantic names for confirmed roles such as angle, gfx_spawn_var, set_sfx_var2, and owner, with extensive comments tying cmd_vars/subaction flags to visibility, shot spawning, put-away behavior, JObj transforms, and effect/SFX triggers. itfoxblaster.c was cleaned up with item_gobj parameter names, Doxygen-style briefs, typed prototypes for spawn functions, and a small apparent SFX addition for Falco blaster put-away. itlgun.c went from effectively empty to 240 lines of item state-table callbacks, spawn/init, attribute extraction, ground/air transitions, timers, animation state changes, collision responses, and pickup/reference handling. Header updates added ItLGunAttr, ItLGunVars, the lgun item-var union member, and more specific item-spawn signatures.
what_changed: Expanded FoxBlasterVars documentation in itCharItems.h, preserving xDD4-style offset names where not fully known while naming confirmed fields: angle at xE5C, gfx_spawn_var at xE74, set_sfx_var2 at xE78, and owner at xE7C.

Added detailed comments mapping Fox blaster subaction command flags to visibility, laser shot spawning, and start/put-away behavior. The comments explicitly call out uncertain fields and flags rather than over-naming them.
Renamed many itfoxblaster.c parameters from arg0-style names to item_gobj and visibility and updated usages of xE74/xE78/xE7C to the new semantic FoxBlasterVars field names.
Added documentation to itfoxblaster.c functions describing JObj visibility/SFX handling, accessory callback redirection, shot effect spawning, root JObj transforms, and blaster spawn routines.
Refined the it_802AE8A8 prototype in it_27CF.h from generic float/HSD_GObj/s32/s32 parameters returning HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj* it_802AE8A8(float, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, Fighter_Part, ItemKind).
Added an extra Item_8026AE84 call with SFX id 0x1AE05 in the Falco/Kirby Falco blaster visibility==2 path, alongside the existing 0x18700 call.

Created a full itlgun.c implementation with ItemStateTable it_803F5CC8 and callbacks for initialization, attribute copy-out, owner notification, ground/air animation changes, gravity/fall handling, timers, collisions, shield/bounce behavior, and reference/pickup handling.
Added ItLGunAttr with fields x0, x4, x8, xC and ItLGunVars with x0 in itCommonItems.h; the PR comment in the diff says the L-Gun attr struct size/types are still uncertain.
Added ItLGunVars lgun to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h so L-Gun state can use item->xDD4_itemVar.lgun.x0.
Added a note in item.c that Item_8026AB54 implementation parameter names do not match the declaration names, documenting a known naming/signature mismatch.
smart_moves: Used semantic names only where the behavior was strongly supported by code paths: gfx_spawn_var is reset after spawning shooting gfx, set_sfx_var2 gates destroy/visibility SFX and JObj flags, and owner is compared against item->owner and passed to fighter helper calls.
Kept unknown FoxBlasterVars fields as xDD4/xDE4/etc. but grouped and commented them by observed behavior. This avoids premature false naming while still making the state machine searchable.
Documented command-variable semantics in place next to the struct fields affected by those cmd_vars, which is useful for future fighter-item cross-reference work.
Converted spawn and callback prototypes to item/fighter-specific types where evidence allowed, improving header clarity and reducing generic HSD_GObj/s32 ambiguity.
Added the L-Gun item-var union member only after implementing code that uses item->xDD4_itemVar.lgun.x0 as a countdown/timer, tying the struct change to concrete accesses.
Preserved matching-sensitive patterns in Fox blaster code, including pointer arithmetic over grouped adjacent struct fields and explicit casts between HSD_GObj* and Item_GObj* in L-Gun callbacks.
Included uncertainty comments, especially around ItLGunAttr size/types and unknown blaster flags, which makes the demystification auditable rather than speculative.
decomp_lessons: For item vars, prefer partial demystification: rename fields with direct behavioral evidence and leave offset names for unknown slots, but add comments describing observed ranges and state transitions.
Subaction cmd_vars can often be decoded by tracking which item vars they set and when those vars are reset. Here, blaster flags were associated with visibility, shot spawning, and start/end actions.
Repeated adjacent struct fields may represent frame-history buffers or grouped state; matching code may require pointer arithmetic over named fields instead of introducing arrays too early.

Item state tables are a useful scaffold for decompiling empty item files: implement init, update, physics, collision, and transition callbacks around Item_80268E5C animation-state changes and common item helpers.
When special attribute structs are uncertain, it is acceptable to add a provisional named attr struct with explicit uncertainty comments and only the fields needed by observed code.

Header signature refinement should be driven by call sites and usage: replacing HSD_GObj*/s32 parameters with Fighter_GObj*, Fighter_Part, ItemKind, and Item_GObj* improves downstream decomp readability.
Documentation can be review-significant in decomp PRs; the only substantive positive reviewer comment was 'Nice documentation!'.
naming_conventions: Use item_gobj for item object parameters instead of arg0 when the callback operates on an item.
Use owner for HSD_GObj* fields that store the owning fighter/item owner object, while keeping owner_gobj for parameters where that distinction matters.
Use visibility for arguments that control visible/present/put-away JObj flag behavior rather than generic arg1.
Use gfx_spawn_var for a flag set by SpecialN code on the shot frame and reset immediately after spawning the shot effect.
Use set_sfx_var2 for the Fox blaster field at xE78 because it gates blaster destroy/put-away SFX behavior, but the name remains somewhat procedural rather than final domain terminology.
Use angle for xE5C where atan2f output is stored and then passed to efSync_Spawn.
Use ItLGunAttr and ItLGunVars for common-item L-Gun structures, with fields kept as x0/x4/x8/xC until their meanings are proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Fox blaster history/group shifting was kept as pointer arithmetic over adjacent struct fields such as xE10/xE0C, xE50/xE44, and xE70/xE6C rather than rewritten as higher-level arrays, likely to preserve matching codegen.
The diff retained explicit casts between HSD_GObj* and Item_GObj* in L-Gun callbacks, matching the mixed callback signatures used by item infrastructure.
A small static inline jobj_child helper was used in itfoxblaster.c to access node->child, likely for readability and possibly codegen control; evidence for matching necessity is weak.
L-Gun uses direct Item_80268E5C animation-state calls with constants 0 through 5 and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE/6 flags, matching item state table transitions.
The L-Gun timer path sets item->xDD4_itemVar.lgun.x0 to 0x28, decrements it each update, and switches animation state when it reaches zero.

Fox blaster transform lookup tables it_803F6D58, it_803F6D90, it_803F6DC8, and it_803F6E28 were left as global/static float arrays with comments tying them to JObj translate/scale/rotate calculations.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.impact: Confirms that the extensive Fox blaster documentation/demystification was valued by review.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether a failure could be ignored or needed fixing, and noted they thought they knew the cause if it was an issue.
review_feedback.impact: There was some CI or build failure discussion, but the excerpt does not identify the failure.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Said that if the issue/failure was in the same file, it was fine.
review_feedback.impact: Suggests a same-file matching or diff failure was acceptable, but evidence is too weak to infer the exact matching policy or affected function.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining FoxBlasterVars xDD4/xDE4/xDFC grouped fields or convert them to arrays/history buffers.
Check whether ItLGunAttr size and field types were later confirmed or renamed beyond x0/x4/x8/xC.
Search for later fixes involving the added Falco blaster Item_8026AE84 SFX id 0x1AE05.
Look for PRs that resolve the Item_8026AB54 declaration/definition parameter-name mismatch noted in item.c.
Compare later itlgun.c changes for improved names of animation states 0-5 and lgun.x0 timer semantics.
Search for review/build comments around same-file failures in PR 1438 if exact matching status is needed.
